content.problemStatement:
Chef has failed miserably in several cooking competitions, which makes him think that he's been out of luck recently. Therefore, he decided to build a magic circle using his NNN chopsticks (numbered 111 through NNN), hoping that it would somehow improve his luck.

The magic circle consists of NNN chopsticks placed on an infinite straight line (so it's not actually a circle). The line is drawn on the ground and chopsticks should be stuck vertically into the ground.

The chopsticks have lengths x1,x2,‚ãØ‚Äâ,xNx_1,x_2,\cdots,x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚ãØ,xN‚Äã. For each valid iii, the iii-th chopstick can be placed on the line at any integer position yiy_iyi‚Äã; then it casts a shadow on the range [yi,yi+xi][y_i,y_i+x_i][yi‚Äã,yi‚Äã+xi‚Äã] on the line. No two chopsticks may be placed at the same position.

The positions of the chopsticks should satisfy an additional condition: for each pair of neighbouring chopsticks iii and jjj (such that there are no other chopsticks between them), the distances between these neighbouring chopsticks ‚à£yi‚àíyj‚à£\left| y_i - y_j \right|‚à£yi‚Äã‚àíyj‚Äã‚à£ must fall in a given range [L,U][L,U][L,U].

The magic power of the circle depends on the total shadowed length S=‚à£‚ãÉi[yi,yi+xi]‚à£S=\left| \bigcup_i [y_i,y_i+x_i] \right|S=‚à£‚ãÉi‚Äã[yi‚Äã,yi‚Äã+xi‚Äã]‚à£. While Chef expects the circle to have the maximum effect, he must prepare for the worst-case scenario ‚Äî due to his recent bad luck. Please help Chef find both the minimum and the maximum value of SSS.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, LLL and UUU.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers x1,x2,‚ãØ‚Äâ,xNx_1,x_2,\cdots,x_Nx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚ãØ,xN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers Smin‚Å°S_{\min}Smin‚Äã and Smax‚Å°S_{\max}Smax‚Äã.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
N‚â§11N \leq 11N‚â§11, and additionally N>5N > 5N>5 in at most one test case

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

To minimize SSS, we may set y1=0y_1=0y1‚Äã=0 and y2=2y_2=2y2‚Äã=2.
To maximize SSS, we may set y1=4y_1=4y1‚Äã=4 and y2=0y_2=0y2‚Äã=0.

Example case 2:

To minimize SSS, we may set y1=2y_1=2y1‚Äã=2, y2=1y_2=1y2‚Äã=1 and y3=0y_3=0y3‚Äã=0.
To maximize SSS, we may set y1=0y_1=0y1‚Äã=0, y2=1y_2=1y2‚Äã=1 and y3=3y_3=3y3‚Äã=3.

Example case 3:

To minimize SSS, we may set y1=0y_1=0y1‚Äã=0, y2=3y_2=3y2‚Äã=3, y3=12y_3=12y3‚Äã=12, y4=6y_4=6y4‚Äã=6 and y5=9y_5=9y5‚Äã=9.
To maximize SSS, we may set y1=0y_1=0y1‚Äã=0, y2=20y_2=20y2‚Äã=20, y3=5y_3=5y3‚Äã=5, y4=10y_4=10y4‚Äã=10 and y5=15y_5=15y5‚Äã=15.
